LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF MARIA JOZAMI'S MOTION FOR APPROVAL OF PAGA SETTLEMENT AND DISMISSAL OF CLASS ACTION WITHOUT PREJUDICE. PLAINTIFF MARIA JOZAMI'S MOTION FOR APPROVAL OF PAGA SETTLEMENT AND DISMISSAL OF CLASS ACTION WITHOUT PREJUDICE IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED, AND GOOD CAUSE SHOWN. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE PROPOSED SETTLEMENT IS FAIR AND ADEQUATE IN VIEW OF THE PURPOSES AND POLICIES OF PAGA, TAKING INTO ACCOUNT SUCH FACTORS AS THE ADEQUACY OF THE CIVIL PENALTIES IN LIGHT OF THE POTENTIAL MAXIMUM IF THE MATTER WERE TO GO TO VERDICT AND THE RISKS OF LITIGATION. (LAB. CODE SEC. 2699(L)(2); S.SEE FLORES V. STARWOOD HOTELS & RESORTS WORLDWIDE, INC. (C.D. CAL. 2017) 253 F.SUPP.3D 1074, 1077; O'CONNOR V. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. (N.D. CAL. 2016) 201 F.SUPP.3D 1110, 1132-1135.) IN ADDITION, THE COURT FINDS THAT THE AMOUNT OF ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS SOUGHT IS REASONABLE. (LAB. CODE SEC. 2699(G)(1).) PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A SEPARATE ORDER AND JUDGMENT BY EMAIL TO CONTESDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG. JUDGE: ETHAN SCHULMAN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-29-2019 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Approval Of Paga Settlement And Dismissal Of Class Action Without Prejudice
Off Calendar Apr-25-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MARIA JOZAMI'S Motion For Approval Of PAGA Settlement And Dismissal Of Class Action Without Prejudice.

Plaintiff Maria Jozami's motion for approval of PAGA settlement and dismissal of class action without prejudice is granted. No opposition filed, and good cause shown. The Court finds that the proposed settlement is fair and adequate in view of the purposes and policies of PAGA, taking into account such factors as the adequacy of the civil penalties in light of the potential maximum if the matter were to go to verdict and the risks of litigation. (Lab. Code Sec. 2699(l)(2); s.See Flores v. Starwood Hotels & Resorts Worldwide, Inc. (C.D. Cal. 2017) 253 F.Supp.3d 1074, 1077; O'Connor v. Uber Technologies, Inc. (N.D. Cal. 2016) 201 F.Supp.3d 1110, 1132-1135.) In addition, the Court finds that the amount of attorney's fees and costs sought is reasonable. (Lab. Code Sec. 2699(g)(1).)

Prevailing party to submit a separate order and judgment by email to contesdept302tr@sftc.org.

Judge: Ethan Schulman; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/EPS)
